title: Online Reviews or a Friend's Advice?

rounds.name: Round 1 · 连接词
rounds.description: 转折 / 并列 / 因果 / 对比 / 让步 / 举例 / 递进 / 视角标记

tokens.v: Claire and Paul both raise valid points,
I think they are framing this as an either-or question
it really shouldn't be.
the right source of advice depends entirely on what is being purchased.
low-stakes, standardized goods — think toothpaste, batteries, or a phone charger — Claire is absolutely right. Review sites let you compare dozens of options in minutes,
quality differences are minimal, there is no need to trouble a friend over something so small.
for complex purchases where personal fit matters, Paul's point becomes far more compelling. Take buying a gaming laptop,
Online reviews may sing the praises of a GPU's benchmark scores,
they cannot tell me whether the specific game I play will actually run smoothly. A friend who shares my hobby,
can
recommend a model suited to my needs
let me test his own computer firsthand — something no website could ever replicate.
I would say: go online
standardized products, ask friends
complex, personalized ones.
